topic.name: Theatre and Performance Arts
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of theatre and performance arts, including historical theatres, contemporary dance adaptations, and their cultural significance.

Document 1: Bristol Old Vic is a British theatre company based at the Theatre Royal, Bristol.
The theatre complex completed the first phase of a £19 million refurbishment.
Document 2: Edward Scissorhands is a contemporary dance adaptation of the 1990 American romance fantasy film.
The piece debuted in London in 2005 and has subsequently toured in Britain, Asia, the U.S., Australia and Europe.

topic.name: Imperial Japanese Navy Warships
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of warships built for and used by the Imperial Japanese Navy, highlighting their roles in significant naval battles and historical events.

Document 1: Asama-class cruisers were built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
They participated in all four main naval battles of the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 05.
Document 2: Mikasa served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War.
Mikasa is the last remaining example of a pre-dreadnought battleship anywhere in the world.
Document 3: The Zuihō class was a group of two aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy before World War II.
Shōhō was the first Japanese aircraft carrier to be sunk during World War II.

topic.name: Michigan State Highways
topic.description: The documents discuss various state trunkline highways in Michigan, detailing their routes, history, and significance.

Document 1: M-188 is a 4.559-mile-long state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan.
M-188 is maintained by the Michigan Department of Transportation (MDOT).
Document 2: M-209 was a state trunkline highway in the lower peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
M-209's designation was 'abandoned' in 1996.

topic.name: Music Albums and Productions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various music albums, their production processes, and the reception they received upon release.

Document 1: Circus is the sixth studio album by American recording artist Britney Spears.
It debuted at number one on the US Billboard 200 with first-week sales of over 505,000 copies.
Document 2: The Christmas Sessions is the first Christmas album by American Christian rock band MercyMe.
The album reached a peak of number three on the Billboard Christian Albums chart.

topic.name: Sports History
topic.description: The documents discuss the historical performance and significance of various sports teams and athletes.

Document 1: Somerset County Cricket Club made their debut in first-class cricket in the 1882 English cricket season.
Wisden Cricketers ' Almanack described the season as 'disastrous'.
Document 2: The Fabulous Kangaroos was a professional wrestling tag team that existed in various forms from 1957 until 1983.
Costello and Heffernan are arguably the most famous version of The Kangaroos, regarded as one of the top tag teams to ever compete in professional wrestling.

topic.name: Sustainable Urban Development
topic.description: The documents focus on strategies and practices for creating environmentally sustainable urban environments.

doc1: strategies for reducing urban carbon footprints
integrating green spaces into city planning
doc2: importance of public transportation in sustainable cities

topic.name: Tropical Storms and Hurricanes
topic.description: The documents discuss the formation, development, and impacts of various tropical storms and hurricanes in the Atlantic region.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Debra was the second of two tropical storms to hit the United States in the 1978 Atlantic hurricane season.
Debra weakened as it moved inland and ultimately dissipated on August 29 over Arkansas.
Document 2: Tropical Storm Danny was a weak and disorganized tropical cyclone that formed in August 2009.
Danny originated in a tropical wave that exited the west coast of Africa on August 18 and entered the Atlantic Ocean.
Document 3: Hurricane Dean, the most intense storm of the 2007 Atlantic hurricane season, formed in the Atlantic Ocean west of Cape Verde.
Dean caused MXN $ 2 billion (US $ 184 million; 2007 dollars) of damage and killed 13 people.

topic.name: Fungi and Their Classification
topic.description: This topic encompasses the study and classification of various fungal species, including their taxonomy, morphology, and ecological roles.

Document 1: Psilocybe yungensis is a species of psychedelic mushroom in the Strophariaceae family.
The species was described as new to science by American mycologists Rolf Singer and Alexander H. Smith.
Document 2: Podoserpula is a genus of fungi in the family Amylocorticiaceae.
The genus contains two species: the type, P. pusio, commonly known as the pagoda fungus.

topic.name: Historic Architectural Sites
topic.description: The documents discuss various historic architectural sites, including churches and castles, highlighting their historical significance and architectural features.

Document 1: St Margaret 's Church is an Anglican church in the Ifield neighbourhood of Crawley
English Heritage has listed the church at Grade I because of its architectural and historical importance.
Document 2: Santa Rosa de la Eminencia castle is a colonial castle built in the seventeenth century
It was declared as a National Monument in 1965.
Document 3: St Catherine 's Castle is a Henrician castle in Cornwall
now managed by English Heritage as a tourist attraction.

topic.name: Sustainable Urban Development
topic.description: The documents discuss strategies and practices for promoting sustainability in urban environments.

topic.name: Video Game Entertainment
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of video games, including their development, gameplay mechanics, and cultural impact.

Document 1: Guitar Hero Smash Hits is a music rhythm game and the fourth expansion game to the Guitar Hero series.
The game features 48 songs originally featured in five previous games in the series.
Document 2: Kingdom Hearts 358 / 2 Days is an action role-playing video game developed by h.a.n.d. and published by Square Enix.
The story is told from the perspective of Roxas, following his daily life within Organization XIII.

topic.name: Political Events and Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss significant political events and notable political figures in various contexts.

Document 1: William J. Fields was a politician from the U.S. state of Kentucky.
He represented Kentucky's Ninth District in the U.S. House of Representatives.
Document 2: The 2007 Slovenian presidential election was held to elect the successor to the second President of Slovenia.
Several political events overshadowed the campaign.

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy sources and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of wind energy on carbon footprint
doc2: renewable energy policies and their effectiveness
case studies on geothermal energy use
doc3: discussion on biofuels and their sustainability

topic.name: Entertainment and Sports
topic.description: This topic encompasses various forms of entertainment and sports, including television shows and adaptive sports achievements.

1: Believe in the Stars is the second episode of the third season of the American television comedy series 30 Rock.
The episode's plot concerns a feud between Tracy Jordan and Jenna Maroney over royalties.
2: Sarah Vinci is a 1 point wheelchair basketball player who plays for the Perth Western Stars.
Vinci represented Australia at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London in wheelchair basketball, winning a silver medal.
3: A hockey pitch is the playing surface for the game of field hockey.
Historically, the game was played on natural turf but nowadays is predominantly played on artificial turf.

topic.name: Military History and Operations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical roles and operations of military units and formations, particularly during significant conflicts such as World War I and World War II.

Document 1: I Corps is a major formation of United States Army Forces Command.
I Corps oversaw US Army divisions as they repelled several major German offensives and led them into Germany.
Document 2: The 25th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the New Zealand Military Forces, which served during the Second World War.
The battalion participated in the Italian Campaign, fighting in actions at Orsogna and later at Cassino.

topic.name: Railway Lines in Norway and the US
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, construction, and operation of significant railway lines in Norway and the United States.

Document 1: 32-kilometer long, single track railway line between Roa and Hønefoss in Norway
The line was built by the Norwegian State Railways and opened on 1 December 1909.
Document 2: high-speed railway line between Oslo and Eidsvoll, Norway
opened in 1998, at the same time as the airport that gave the line its name.
Document 3: American passenger train run by the Baltimore and Ohio Railroad
inaugurated on May 12, 1923, as an all-Pullman sleeping car train running from Pennsylvania Station in New York City to Chicago.

topic.name: Notable Figures and Events
topic.description: This cluster discusses significant individuals and events related to science, politics, and popular culture.

Document 1: Raemer Edgar Schreiber was an American physicist from McMinnville, Oregon who served Los Alamos National Laboratory during World War II, participating in the development of the atomic bomb.
He served as deputy director of the laboratory from 1972 until his retirement in 1974.
Document 2: Alexander Litvinenko was a former officer of the Russian Federal Security Service (FSB) and KGB, who fled from court prosecution in Russia and received political asylum in the United Kingdom.
Litvinenko's allegations about the misdeeds of the FSB and his public deathbed accusations that Russian president Vladimir Putin was behind his unusual malady resulted in worldwide media coverage.
Document 3: Barnard 'Barney' Gumble is a fictional character on the American animated sitcom The Simpsons.
Barney was inspired by the cartoon character Barney Rubble from The Flintstones and by several barflies from other television programs.

topic.name: Horse and Sheep Breeds
topic.description: This topic covers various horse and sheep breeds, their origins, characteristics, and historical significance.

Document 1: Morgan horse is one of the earliest horse breeds developed in the United States.
Morgans served many roles in 19th-century American history.
Document 2: The Canadian horse is a horse breed from Canada.
During the peak popularity of the breed, three subtypes could be distinguished.
Document 3: The Boreray is a breed of sheep originating on the St Kilda archipelago.
The breed is the rarest breed of sheep in the United Kingdom.

topic.name: Cultural and Culinary Traditions
topic.description: This cluster explores various cultural practices and innovations, including political leadership, culinary creations, and garden design.

Document 1: Fredrik Reinfeldt was Prime Minister of Sweden from 2006 to 2014
Reinfeldt's leadership transformed the Moderate Party's policies.
Document 2: A Bacon Explosion is a pork dish that consists of bacon wrapped around a filling of spiced sausage
The Bacon Explosion became an Internet phenomenon, generating more than 500,000 hits.

topic.name: Canadian Science Fiction Magazines
topic.description: The documents discuss the history and publication of Canadian science fiction pulp magazines during the early 1940s.

Document 1: Uncanny Tales was a Canadian science fiction pulp magazine edited by Melvin R. Colby that ran from November 1940 to September 1943.
It was created in response to the wartime reduction of imports on British and American science-fiction pulp magazines.
Document 2: Comet was a pulp magazine which published five issues from December 1940 to July 1941.
It was edited by F. Orlin Tremaine, who had edited Astounding Stories, one of the leaders of the science fiction magazine field.

topic.name: Mathematics and Physics Concepts
topic.description: The documents discuss fundamental concepts in mathematics and physics, including logarithms, particle interactions, and linguistic roots.

Document 1: Binary logarithm ( log2 n ) is the power to which the number 2 must be raised to obtain the value n.
The binary logarithm function is the inverse function of the power of two function.
Document 2: The weak interaction is one of the four known fundamental interactions of nature.
The theory of the weak interaction is sometimes called quantum flavordynamics ( QFD ).
Document 3: The roots of the reconstructed Proto-Indo-European language ( PIE ) are basic parts of words that carry a lexical meaning.
Typically, a root plus a suffix forms a stem, and adding an ending forms a word.

topic.name: Cultural and Artistic Expressions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various forms of cultural and artistic expressions, including religious texts, music compositions, and sound design innovations.

Document 1: Greek minuscule manuscript of the New Testament
text of the four Gospels
Document 2: musical setting of the Latin text of the Magnificat
Bach's setting was written for a performance on 2 July
Document 3: American audio engineer
developed the first effective automatic microphone mixer

topic.name: Legal and Social Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant legal reforms and social advocacy efforts in the context of military organization and reproductive health services.

Document 1: Territorial and Reserve Forces Act 1907 reformed the auxiliary forces of the British Army
The reorganisation formed a major part of the Haldane Reforms
Document 2: Ronald Marc George is the retired 27th Chief Justice of California
George argued unsuccessfully on behalf of the State of California before the United States Supreme Court
Document 3: Planned Parenthood provides reproductive health services in the United States and around the world
PPFA is the largest single provider of reproductive health services, including abortion, in the United States

topic.name: Christian Movements and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses various Christian movements and their historical reforms, focusing on their beliefs, practices, and the challenges they faced.

Document 1: Christian movement established in Besançon, France
belonging to the Pentecostal movement
Document 2: Anglo-Saxon Archbishop of Canterbury
reforming the clergy of his cathedral
Document 3: related to Mormonism, the principal branch of the Latter Day Saint movement
Mormons dedicate large amounts of time and resources to serving in their church

topic.name: Innovations in Technology and Media
topic.description: This topic encompasses advancements in technology and media, highlighting the intersection of engineering, entertainment, and architecture.

Document 1: NuScale Power designs and markets small modular reactors (SMRs).
NuScale is currently planning the first NuScale power plant in Idaho.
Document 2: The Legally Prohibited from Being Funny on Television Tour was a comedy tour by American comedian and talk show host Conan O'Brien.
O'Brien announced on March 11, 2010 via his Twitter account that he would embark on a 30-city live tour.
Document 3: One Times Square is a 25 story, 363-foot-high skyscraper located at 42nd Street and Broadway in New York City.
The tower was originally built to serve as the headquarters of the local newspaper, The New York Times.
